notes: Unitree founder Wang Xingxing stated at the 2026 Yabuli Entrepreneurs Forum that humanoid robots could break the 10-second 100m barrier by mid-2026. Unitree's H1 was recorded at a peak 10.1 m/s during an April 2026 internal sprint test.

target_metric: 10 m/s peak running speed (capability claim, not a parity target)
notes: MirrorMe Technology unveiled the Bolt humanoid on February 2, 2026 with a claimed peak running speed of 10 m/s (~22.4 mph) in real-world testing. Not a sanctioned event attempt, no timed 100 m performance on a standardized track yet. Source URL substituted from the Commit 10 spec's reference (which pointed at a Unitree article) to the CnEVPost coverage of MirrorMe's own announcement.

target_metric: Fully autonomous (no handler intervention, no battery swaps, no course corrections) sub-human-world-record half marathon
notes: Honor's Lightning won the 2026 Beijing E-Town event at 50:26 under autonomous navigation but with handler interventions, battery swaps, and course corrections that kept the result ineligible against World Athletics rules. A fully-compliant autonomous repeat (no interventions) is an inferred open target, not a verbatim Honor commitment — flagged as such in the notes. When a clearer Honor public statement exists, update this entry's source_url and source_type.
